author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
Inconsistency of Giving the Soul All the Parts of Sex and Yet No Sex.
title: Inconsistency of Giving the Soul All the Parts of Sex and Yet No Sex.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: For that form of the soul, whether masculine or feminine, which has the distinction of members characteristic of man and woman, being no semblance merely of body, but actual body, is either a male or a female, whether you will or no, precisely as it appears to be a man or a woman.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: But if your opinion be correct, and the soul is a body, even a living body, then it both possesses swelling and pendent breasts, and lacks a beard, it has a womb, and all the generative organs of a woman, yet is not a woman after all.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Will not mine, then, be a statement more consistent with truth: the soul, indeed, has an eye and has a tongue, has a finger, and all other members which resemble those of the body, and yet the whole is the semblance of a body, not a body really?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: My statement is open to a general test; everybody can prove it in himself, when he brings home to his mind the image of absent friends; he can prove it with certainty when he recalls the figures both of himself and other persons, which have occurred to him in his dreams.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: On your part, however, no example can throughout nature be produced of such a monstrosity as you have imagined, where there is a woman’s real and living body, but not a woman’s sex.
